task.description: Grade each item by comparing the current answers with Correct Answers in the same folder.

inputs.model_answers: The answers produced by the answerer (current answers file)
inputs.correct_answers: The reference answers located in the same folder

grading_criteria.description: Treat an answer as correct if ANY of the following conditions is true.

conditions.name: normalized_exact_match
conditions.rule: After lower-casing and stripping leading/trailing spaces and punctuation, the strings match exactly.

conditions.name: verbatim_substring
conditions.rule: The model_answer contains the entire correct phrase verbatim, even if extra words appear before or after.

conditions.name: parenthetical_acronym_difference
conditions.rule: The only difference is a parenthetical acronym/abbreviation.

conditions.name: generic_filler_noun_difference
conditions.rule: The model_answer adds or drops a generic filler noun.

conditions.name: expert_synonym_judgment
conditions.rule: In your expert judgment the terms are true synonyms in standard audiology usage.

per_item_line.ordering: Original order of items must be preserved.

summary_line.accuracy_rule: Accuracy is a whole-number percentage rounded to the nearest integer.

strict_rules: Use exactly '✅' for correct and '❌' for incorrect.
Do NOT add explanations beyond what is specified.
Accuracy must be a whole-number percentage rounded to the nearest integer.
output_examples: 2. ❌  (FIB) – expected: probe microphone, got: real-ear microphone
